feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Does a SAC workspace functions like private files?
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I am currently setting up SAC and would like to use the workspaces to clearly separate different departments. Additionally, I want to restrict permissions for private files so that work can only be done in public files.

I have now noticed that when the permissions for private files are removed, there are no files left in the workspace. I assume that a workspace functions like private files. Is that correct?

As a solution, I am left with the option of defining custom roles for the workspace teams, right?

Best regards,
Niels

entry.title: SAC settlement time variance best practices
entry.content:
Hi, 
Is there any best practice for a reconciliation model including a calculation of a settlement time variance. something like that:

Settlement Variance=Actual Settlement Time−Expected Settlement Time

Thanks

entry.title: Does anyone have a workaround for null suppression in a waterfall graph?
entry.content:
In SAP Analytics Cloud I want to remove measures from a waterfall that are null. Given filtering on e.g. profit centers, a measure might end up being null and if the filter changes, the measure might take on a positive or negative value and should then be included again.

Datasource is a SAP Datasphere Analytical Model.

Does anyone have a workaround for null suppression in a waterfall graph? As per KBA 3150383 this is not available even though it is possible to enable zero suppression (which does not seem to work either, see screen shots). A couple of screen shots has been attached for context. 

SAC table of datasource without zero or null suppression.

Same table WITH zero and null suppression. All ok.

Waterfall WITH zero suppression. I want to get rid of the highlighted elements. For some reason, Consulting Fee Recovery is not removed with zero suppression.

Thanks for your help.

entry.title: SAC Date Calculation in DA
entry.content:
I have a “payment method” dimension with an attribute for “Expected Settlement Time”. Based on the method chosen the attribute can be 1/2/3/4 days (the attribute is set as an integer).

I have a measure for incomes, a measure for outcomes and one created to copy the income in the expected settlement date, named “Expected Outgoing”.

My problem is that I don’t know how to write this calculation - copying the some 3-4 days after based on the attribute.

I’ve tried the below, but the syntax isn’t correct, as it mixes date and number of days:

DATA([d/Measures] = "Expected_Outgoing") = 
RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Income", [d/Date] = DATEDIFF( [d/Date],[d/IL_D_POC_RPD_PMNTMTHD].[p/STLMNT_TIME],"DAY" ) )

DATA([d/Measures] = "Expected_Outgoing") =

RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Income", [d/Date] = DATEDIFF( [d/Date], [d/IL_D_POC_RPD_PMNTMTHD].[p/STLMNT_TIME], "DAY" ) )

 

How should I approach that?

entry.title: Fill Parameter Year in Data Action using setParameterValue
entry.content:
Hi,

i'm facing an issue trying to pass a parameter in a data action using setParameterValue. I need to pass a year, as you can do it with the data action trigger.

I've seen that a similar problem has already been discussed, but in my situation, the solution proposed it's not working: 
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sac-fill-parameter-year-in-data-action-using-analytic-app-script/qaq-p/12606009

Am i missing something?

Kind regards,

Enrico

entry.title: Fetch a dimension member based on a property value using Advanced Formulas
entry.content:
Hi experts

Is there a way in advanced formulas to identify a member ID based on a property value?

I have 2 models: Model A (dimensions: account, profit, channel) and Model B (account, profit). I need to copy data from Model A to Model B. The account dimension for model B should be the ID value for account dimension based on the property "Channel". 

For example: 

Any advice?

entry.title: Analytics cloud for office - SAC
entry.content:
Hello,

  1. Is there an additional cost for using the Analytics Cloud for Office add-in in Microsoft Excel, as it mentions "Additional purchase may be required" (see the screenshot below) when trying to download?

  2. Since an email ID (MS Office 365) account is required to download the add-in, how is this linked to the SAP SAC license?

Regards,

Vijai Muniraj

entry.title: SAC Settlement time deviation
entry.content:
Hi everybody, 

and special thanks to @N1kh1l  & @MoonJun for your previous and precious help.

I'm trying to do the below and I'm not using correctly the advanced formulas with date.

I have 2 measures Incoming amount and Outgoing amount. I want to calculate the Settlement time deviation.
For that, I use a status dimension to move my incomes, my expected outcomes and actual outcomes in a "comparison layer"
As a result, I have 3 dates for the same transaction, and I need to compare the expected outcomes and actual outcomes and calculate the deviation. 
In the screenshot bellow, transaction 1's income was on January 4th, the expected settlement was on the 6th, in fact it was settled on the 7th - what I look for is 1 day of deviation.
the code I'm using is wrong:

 

 

DATA([d/Measures]="Time_deviation")=
	RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="Outgoing",[d/IL_D_POC_RPD_STATUS]="STS_023",DAY([d/Date]))-
	RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="Income",[d/IL_D_POC_RPD_STATUS]="STS_023",DAY([d/Date]))

 

I've also tried the below and yet I don't do it right

INTEGER @DAYINC
INTEGER @DAYOUT

FOREACH[d/Date]
	@DAYINC= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="Income", [d/IL_D_POC_RPD_STATUS]="STS_023")
	@DAYOUT= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="Outgoing", [d/IL_D_POC_RPD_STATUS]="STS_023")
	
	DATA([d/Measures]="Time_deviation", [d/IL_D_POC_RPD_STATUS]="STS_023",[d/Date]="2025-01-01")=
	DATEDIFF(@DAYINC , @DAYOUT,"DAY" )
ENDFOR

 



Thank you!

entry.title: SAP Content Network (Transfer between two clients / Data Storage) Query
entry.content:
I am wondering the following scenario.

The customer already has a Productive and Test SAC Tenant.

1. To make organisational changes and separate the Data, the customer is considering getting another Productive and Test client. Is it possible to use the export/import SAP Content Network to do the transfer of objects from old (Test/Productive) to new (Test/Productive) clients?

2. During the data transfer, what type of data is stored in the SAP Content Network? Is it just the metadata or any additional data as well?

3. Are Planning Applications transported differently?

entry.title: SAC Calculation Date Input Controls
entry.content:
Hi ,
I'm relatively new to SAC and I'm currently working on a dashboard which has two input controls - Current Date range and Comparison Date range for users to select. Comparison date range governs all the PoP (period over Period) variances in the report.Report is created as a 'Story' and the model is a analytical model from SAP Datasphere.User would like the date input controls current date range to be defaulted to 'last week' dynamically and comparison input control to be last year with the flexibility to change the dates if required. I have tried to make it dynamic but it is not serving the purpose as it not letting me change the dates.  Please let me know if there any work arounds to achieve this .Thanks!

entry.title: We need text value like more or less, but instead of that we are getting 1 or 0 like measures.
entry.content:
We need text value like "more" or "less", but instead of that we are getting 1 or 0 like measures. We are creating a story, where have created table where we have to get the value in text like more or less, instead we are getting in number format like 0 or 1, Attaching two image, we need result like 1st image, but in 2nd image we are getting 0 and 1. please provide the solution.


Image1 - What we expect

Image2 - What we are getting

entry.title: Problems with random payload from tables in tabs
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a Classic Analytic Application with 5 Tabstrip Tabs. Every Tab has Charts and Tables. All Charts and Tables in all Tabs are configured with RefreshPausedAPI = Always Pause except Tab 1, because it's the Startup-Tab. 

After ~22sec all Charts in Tab 1 are loaded, but Chrome Dev Tools Network Tab still shows Payload, also Windows Task Manager has High CPU Usage from 0-42sec, although Tab 1 is finished at 22sec.

If I switch to another Tab (Tab 2) at 22sec the loading time of Tab 2 is about 15sec. If I wait 42sec and switch then to Tab 2 the loading time is only 5sec. So something is going on between 22-42sec that I cant see and which has nothing to do with Tab 1.

I checked Network Tab in Dev Tools and saw that some Tables create a Payload. Strangely only a few. In Tab 4 I got 2 Tables. One of them creates a Payload (Screenshot) and the other one doesnt. In Tab 5 I got 9 tables - 2 create a Payload, 7 dont. All tables are basically exactly the same. 

What is going on between 22-42sec where CPU Usage is still very high that the Dashboard is not really usable?

entry.title: Using SAC Rest API Patch /api/v1/permissions - Has anyone been able to use it?
entry.content:
Hello,

According to SAP Business Accelerator Hub, we should be able to use this API to mass-change object access.

However, the documentation is not adequately defined and there is no example of a body for the PATCH request.

Has anyone been able to find the correct specification?

Even if you "Try out" SAP forgot to update the API.

SAP Analytics Cloud API

entry.title: Full Time Employee Planning - Calculation of FTE YTD
entry.content:
Hello all, 

I’m encountering an issue while calculating Full-Time Employee (FTE) Year-to-Date (YTD). My dataset includes only two years: 2018 and 2019.

Initially, I calculated FTE Actual using the following formula:

DRV0220 FTE Actual:LOOKUP([DRV0100], [d/VERSION].[p/VERSION] = "Actual" and [d/TIME] = "201906")

However, when implementing this in the SAC Account dimension, I had to make some adjustments to get the correct values, using:LOOKUP([DRV0100], [d/VERSION].[p/VERSION] = "Actual" and [d/TIME] = "2019"."20192")

Similarly, for calculating Opening Initial, I initially used:

FTE0010 Opening Initial:IF([d/TIME].[p/CALMONTH] = "201906", [DRV0220], 0)

But in SAC, I had to modify it as follows to get the expected results:IF([d/TIME].[h/YQM] = ("2019", "2019"."20192", "2019"."20192"."201906"), [DRV0220], 0) The issue arises when applying the final logic for FTE YTD:

DRV0210 FTE YTD: LOOKUP([DRV0200], [d/TIME] = Current("Year").LastPeriods("Month", 24))No values are appearing. I’m unsure what mistake I might be making. Could you help identify any necessary adjustments? I've attached a screenshot for reference.

entry.title: SAC - Uploading Dimension with Append
entry.content:
Hey
I have a dimension with a hierarchy that needs to be partially manually maintained.
 however, every time I refresh the ETL, I lose the manual adjustments made.
is there a way to upload only new members to a dimension?

Thanks

entry.title: Is there a hard limit on the number bookmarks in SAP analytics cloud stories?
entry.content: Is there a hard limit on the number bookmarks in SAP analytics cloud stories?

entry.title: Filter by fixed date range does not allow selection unless I edit the filter every month
entry.content:
1) I understand the values in the dataset drive the range of available values...

2) editing the date range every month is a bit annoying...

At the beginning of each month, I must go in and edit the date range filter on my story to include that month.  The date range filter is set to FIXED, because the DYNAMIC filter does not provide the SLIDER.

Dynamic

Any suggestions?

James

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud | Hide Story Buffering Screen
entry.content:
Dear all,

I am currently working on a seemingly easy SAC problem. My question is if it is posible to hide the SAC story load buffering screen, when opening a story:

So instead of seeing the above it should be blank.

Reason for that: I have created a landing page with a navigation and I would like to create the impression that everything is part of an integrated tool. is it possible to achieve this look and feel. If so, how could you achieve that? I have tried the Application.hideBusyIndicator(), but that did not do anything.

Thank you very much in advance!

entry.title: Inquiry on Seamless Planning Activation and HANA Cloud Verification in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I would like to ask if the seamless planning functionality is currently active or if it is planned for a future release. I'm trying to utilize it, but I don't see the option to select the "Data Storage Location" when creating a new model in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). Additionally, I am unsure if my SAC system is running on HANA Cloud, which might be part of the issue. Could you advise how I can confirm whether my SAC system is running on HANA Cloud?

Please let me know if seamless planning is active for any tenants or if this feature will be released at a later date.

Thank you for your assistance!

entry.title: SAC interview questions
entry.content:
For preparing SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). 

Questions:

1). What is SAC?

A cloud-based analytics tool by SAP for data analysis, visualization, and decision-making.

SAP analytics cloud is end-to-end cloud solution offered by SAP that integrates Business Intelligence , Planning, Augmented analytics, predictive analytics in a unified platform to make effective decision on organization's data. Basically, a SaaS that combines all analytical capability in a single product for all its users

2). What is Model in SAC?

 "Model" refers to the structured representation of data that is imported or connected to SAC for analysis, visualization, and reporting purposes.

It includes the data schema, dimensions, measures, and relationships necessary for analyzing and visualizing the data effectively within SAC.

 

3). Types of Model?

Live Data Connection :

model establishes a direct connection to an external data source, such as SAP HANA, SAP BW, or other databases. It allows SAC to query and analyze data in real-time without importing it into SAC.

Imported Data Model :

 type of model, data is imported from external sources into SAC and stored within the SAC environment. This allows for offline analysis and provides more flexibility in data manipulation and modeling within SAC.

4).What do you understand by digital boardroom in SAP Analytics Cloud?

A single presentation in the SAP analytics cloud combines multiple stories. hey give an overview of a business with summaries of different sections in one place.

5). How is smart prediction performed in SAP Analytics Cloud?

 Smart prediction is made with the help of SAP Machine Learning algorithms. The ML algorithms dig out the relationships between different data and build a formula known as a predictive scenario. We use this formula to predict future trends.'

Three predictive scenarios: regression, classification, and times series

6). What is Smart Predict

Smart Predict uses SAP machine learning algorithms to explore relationships in your dataset and build a formula – a Predictive Scenario – to predict future events or trends

7). What is  Smart Insights

With Smart Insights features, business users uncover critical hidden insights from data previously unseen.

 

 8). What is Smart Discovery

Smart Discovery gives an overview of your data by automatically building charts to begin discovering more about your data.

 

9). What is Search to insights

“Search to Insight” is one such feature that brings conversational AI to SAP Analytics Cloud wherein you can create a story by asking questions in natural language.

10). What are dimensions in SAC?

dimensions are attributes that provide context and structure to your data. They typically represent qualitative data, such as product names, customer

11). What are Measures in SAC?

Measures are numerical values representing quantitative data that can be analyzed, aggregated, and compared.

12). What are the types of filters in SAC?

In SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), there are several types of filters that users can apply to analyze data:

Story, page, widgets

—Dimension Filters: Allow users to filter data based on specific values or ranges within dimensions, such as product categories, regions, or time periods.

—Measure Filters :Enable users to filter data based on specific numerical values or ranges within measures, such as sales revenue, quantities sold, or profit margins.

—Time Filters: Specifically designed for filtering data based on time-related dimensions, such as dates, months, quarters, or years. Users can apply time filters to analyze data within specific time periods.

—Input Controls: Provide interactive filtering options, such as dropdown lists, checkboxes, or sliders, allowing users to select specific values or ranges dynamically.

—Page Filters:Allow users to create multiple pages within a story or report, each with its set of filters applied to analyze data from different perspectives.

—Rank Filters :Enable users to filter data based on ranked values, such as top or bottom performers within a dataset.

 

—Advanced Filters: Provide more complex filtering options, allowing users to create custom filter conditions using expressions or formulas.

These filters help users customize their data analysis and focus on relevant information based on their specific requirements.

 

 

 

# SAC
